description: Per-tenant rate limit policy. Bounds the cost (in 'tokens' — abstract unit, e.g. one reasoning op = 1 token by default) any single tenant can spend across reasoning-backend operations within a rolling window. Prevents one tenant exhausting shared LLM quota and starving others. Enforcement is a token-bucket algorithm: each consume() either decrements the bucket or returns retry_after_ms. Buckets persist to disk so multi-process workers share state.
rationale: Per-tenant rate limits are a baseline isolation control. They do not replace upstream provider quotas (Anthropic/Claude/Gemini) which Kyberion does not own — but they prevent one tenant burning through a shared API key faster than others. burst_capacity allows brief spikes; denial_grace_ms is the soft delay before a denied caller may retry.
